title: Lumen — Business Model and Go-to-Market
content:
# brain/lumen-business.md — Business Model & Go-to-Market
> Last updated: 2026-03-10

## Business Model

### Two-Tier Subscription

| | Tier 1 — Local | Tier 2 — Cloud |
|---|---|---|
| Audio path | P2P WebRTC → GM's machine | WebRTC → Lumen media server |
| Transcription | Local `faster-whisper` on GM's GPU | Cloud Whisper (`large-v3`) on Lumen GPU instances |
| GPU required? | Yes (6+ GB NVIDIA VRAM) | No |
| Privacy | Audio never leaves GM's machine | Audio processed in memory, never stored |
| Target user | Power users, privacy-conscious, beefy rigs | Laptops, Mac, AMD, wants best accuracy |
| Price | Free or $3–5/month | $12–20/month |
| Server cost to Lumen | ~$10–20/mo flat (signaling + TURN) | $6–20/mo per GM (GPU compute) + base infra |

Both tiers share: Hub UI, suggestion engine, memory, conflict detection, Foundry integration, Campaign Ledger, post-session mode, VoIP client. Players don't know which tier the GM uses. [EXPLICIT]

### Revenue Logic

- Local tier is a **funnel** — gets GMs using the product at low/no cost [EXPLICIT]
- Cloud tier is the **revenue driver** — removes hardware barrier, justifies premium pricing [EXPLICIT]
- BYO API keys for suggestions means Lumen doesn't absorb LLM inference costs [EXPLICIT]
- Foundry module is free — lowers adoption barrier [EXPLICIT]

### Key Pricing Constraint

Cloud tier subscription must cover GPU compute per subscriber with margin. If average compute cost per GM approaches subscription price, the model is unsustainable at small scale. [EXPLICIT]

## Cost Modeling (SPECULATIVE — Replace with PoC Data)

These estimates are pre-PoC. Phase 1 benchmarks + a post-PoC cloud cost test will produce real numbers.

| Variable | Estimate | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Avg session length | 3–4 hours | Medium | Typical PF2e session length [ASSUMED] |
| Sessions per GM per month | 4–5 | Medium | Weekly game night [ASSUMED] |
| GPU compute per session-hour | $0.50–1.00 | Low | Cloud GPU pricing estimates [INFERRED] |
| Compute cost per GM per month | $6–20 | Low | Derived from above [INFERRED] |
| Media server + signaling base cost | $20–50/month | Medium | Small VPS + coturn [INFERRED] |
| Break-even subscribers (cloud tier at $15/mo) | 5–15 to cover base infra | Low | Depends on compute cost [INFERRED] |

### Break-Even Scenarios

| Compute/GM/month | Subscription | Margin/GM | Break-even on base infra ($30/mo) |
|---|---|---|---|
| $6 | $15 | $9 | ~4 subscribers |
| $10 | $15 | $5 | ~6 subscribers |
| $15 | $15 | $0 | ❌ Underwater |
| $6 | $20 | $14 | ~3 subscribers |
| $10 | $20 | $10 | ~3 subscribers |
| $15 | $20 | $5 | ~6 subscribers |

**Action required:** Post-PoC, deploy Whisper on a cloud GPU (RunPod or Modal), process a 3-hour recorded session, and measure actual compute cost. This replaces all estimates above. [EXPLICIT]

## Market

### Target User
- PF2e Game Masters running Foundry VTT v13 [EXPLICIT]
- Technically comfortable enough to install Foundry and manage a VTT [INFERRED]
- Willing to pay for GM tooling (many already pay for Foundry license, VPS hosting, content packs) [INFERRED]
- PF2e community is fiercely loyal to tools built specifically for PF2e vs. system-agnostic [INFERRED]

### Market Size (Unknown — Needs Research)
- Foundry VTT user base: unknown exact numbers. Active subreddit, active Discord [ASSUMED]
- PF2e is the second most popular TTRPG system after D&D 5e [ASSUMED]
- PF2e + Foundry overlap is a dedicated niche but not huge — this is a focused product, not a mass-market app [INFERRED]

### Competitive Landscape
- **D&D Beyond** — owned by WotC, D&D 5e only, no VTT audio integration, no real-time transcription [EXPLICIT]
- **Foundry VTT modules** — various community modules for journaling, session notes, etc. None do real-time transcription or AI suggestions [INFERRED]
- **Generic transcription tools** (Otter.ai, etc.) — not TTRPG-aware, no speaker attribution for voice chat, no Foundry integration [INFERRED]
- **No direct competitor** doing real-time transcription + AI GM assistance + Foundry integration for PF2e [INFERRED]

### Differentiation
- VoIP-first with deterministic speaker attribution (no one else does this for TTRPGs) [EXPLICIT]
- PF2e-native — uses PF2e rules compendium, not generic [EXPLICIT]
- Foundry VTT integration (direct journal writes, actor updates) [EXPLICIT]
- Campaign memory with conflict detection [EXPLICIT]
- Local-first option with strong privacy story [EXPLICIT]

## Go-to-Market (Speculative — Post-PoC)

### Channels
- PF2e Foundry Discord community [INFERRED]
- r/Pathfinder2e and r/FoundryVTT subreddits [INFERRED]
- Foundry VTT package listing (free connector module) [EXPLICIT]
- Word of mouth in actual-play and TTRPG communities [ASSUMED]

### Launch Strategy Options (Decide Post-PoC)

**(a) Local tier only** — ship fast, minimal infra, prove the product. Add cloud later.
- Pros: fastest to market, lowest cost, strongest privacy story
- Cons: narrow hardware audience, no premium revenue at launch

**(b) Both tiers simultaneously** — more engineering but wider market at launch.
- Pros: captures both power users and laptop GMs immediately
- Cons: more infrastructure to build and maintain, higher launch cost

**(c) Cloud-first** — if local tier proves too fragile across diverse GPU hardware.
- Pros: simplest user experience (no GPU concerns)
- Cons: weakest privacy story, highest infrastructure cost, no free tier

Cole's current leaning: local tier first, cloud tier later [INFERRED from "ship local, prove the product" discussion]

### Paizo Relationship
- Cole initially considered selling Lumen to Paizo [EXPLICIT]
- This was pushed back on — Paizo is a publisher, not a software company. They don't acquire desktop apps with real-time audio processing [EXPLICIT]
- The right model is a standalone subscription product serving the PF2e community directly [EXPLICIT]
- Paizo partnership (endorsement, licensing, API access) could be valuable later but is not required [INFERRED]

## Open Business Questions

- [ ] What's the actual addressable market size? (PF2e GMs on Foundry with compatible hardware)
- [ ] Price sensitivity: would GMs pay $15/mo? $20/mo? (Post-PoC community survey)
- [ ] What hardware do PF2e Foundry GMs actually have? (Survey to validate spec tiers)
- [ ] Cloud compute cost per session — is the margin viable? (Post-PoC benchmark)
- [ ] Freemium vs. flat local tier pricing? (Free local builds the funnel faster but generates no revenue)

---
*Update cost estimates with real data after PoC benchmarking. Tag confidence levels on all facts.*
